ety: S. पत्त्त्र+कः; Prk. पत्तं, or पत्तओ
def: s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring
more: n. m. 1. A leaf (of a tree). 2. A card. 1. Wohī ḍhāk ke tīn pāt . Prov. The Dhāk stem has but three leaves. (Do what I will I can never get any more—usually said by domestics and subordinate officers whose pay is never raised). 2. Koī hukm bin uske chaltā nahiṅ, Kahīṅ ek pattā bhī hiltā nahīṅ . Sohan Lal. Without his leave nothing can move, No leaf can stir of tree or grove. 3. A leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring. Bālī, patte, jhūmke! Street cry. pattā toṛ bhāgnā . v. n. To run as fast as one's legs will carry him; take to one's heels. (So called from a boy's game called ātī pātī , q. v. in which the runner is required to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have scampered off). Rupayā lete hī pattā-toṛ bhāg gayā .

n. m. S. पत्र leaf, पट्ट a board. 1. A board on which Hindus sit while eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies (More com. paṭrā ). 2. A fuller's board. 3. A dog or horse collar. 4. The gold or silver threads of an old native shoe. 5. A shoulder strap and badge. 6. A deed, commonly a title deed to land, or a lease. 7. A code or book of regulations for the guidance of rent-collectors. 8. Unripe grain (Cicer arietinum) . E. Āi tohar beṭā bahut jiyān kaëlak, paṭṭā chhīmī toṛī lāel . Tir. Your son has done great damage today, he has gone and plucked a lot of unripe peas. paṭṭā utārnā , v. a. To discharge from service. paṭṭā-pher , n. m. The conclusion of a Hindu marriage ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom change seats. paṭṭā tuṛānā , v. n. To get loose; to run away. Kuttā paṭṭā tuṛā gayā. paṭṭā ṭheke-dārī , n. A farming lease.
s. m. 1. A leaf. 2. A trinket. پتاهونا pattā honā, To run away, to flee.
subs: v.n. To run as fast as one can, to take to one's heels (so called from a boy's game in which the runner has to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have run off)
v.n. To run off, run away, flee.

more: n. f. S. पत्रावली 1. Leaves sewn together and serving as a plate to eat out of. 2. A plate of cakes, laḍḍū , and other sweetmeats, etc. 1. Byāh pīchhe pattal bhārī . Prov. The marriage feast done with and o'er, a crust of bread to give is dolour sore. 2. Khāyā, pattal phāṛ ḍālī. pattal bāṅdh'nā , v. a. To bar the feast against the guests. It consists in barring the guests from beginning their meal, till one of their number shall have given the answer to a riddle which is proposed by one of the women. The answer is called pattal kholnā , i. e. permitting the guests to sit to the meal. 1. Ujhak jharokan sundar nārī, dehī sakal sajnan ko gārī, Bāṅdhī pattal bhog lagāve, mūrakh panchan māṅh kahāve . Wom. The fair ones sprang and from the windows showered abuse on all, 'The meal is barred—a fool is he who on the food will fall.' 2. Sirī gur-deo Basisṭ soṅ kahī Dasrath yeh bāt, 'Pattal kholo p…
s. f. A plate or trencher formed of leaves.
subs: v.n. To withhold or bar the meal or repast (this is done till one of the guests shall have solved the riddle which is proposed by one of the women of the party)
v.n. To open the repast, commence the meal (on the solution of the proposed riddle).

The part between the fore-finger and thumb, sacred to the manes. pitr-dān, pitr-ārpan , n. m. Gifts in honor of one's deceased ancestors. pitr-shar…

def: Judeo-Urdu spelling of پَتَّھر (patthar, “stone”)
1. s. m. Name of a sort of greens. 2. A skinflint. 3. Name of a serpent. 4. Name of a fish. 5. adj. One attached to his own house, who remains there even in distress.
s. (dakh.) Lichen rotundatus, Lin. or rock lichen ( Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. lxiv).
s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
more: n. m. S. पत्थर 1. A stone; rockstone. 2. A millstone. 1. Patthar ko joṅk nahīṅ lagtī , Prov. No leech sticks to a stone. 2. Mom ho to pigle, kahīṅ patthar bhī piglā hai? Prov. Wax would melt, but could you ever melt a stone? (Said of the miserly and hard-hearted). 3. A precious stone; jewel; gem; diamond. 4. A hailstone. 5. Anything hard, troublesome, disgusting, intolerable, etc. patthar , adj. 1. Hard; inflexible; admantine; hard as iron. 2. Hard-hearted; cruel; pitiless; inexorable. 3. Heavy; burdensome; immoveable; intolerable. Merī chhātī par do patthar baiṭhe haiṅ . Two heavy stones lie on my breast. (Two daughters to marry). 4. Dull; idle; lazy. 5. Stupid; foolish. 6. Hard; difficult; puzzling; intricate. 7. Hateful; odious; abominable; disgusting.

s. m. A stone, stone. پتهربازي patthar-bāzī, s. f. Stone-throwing, playing with stones. پتهرپاني هوجانا patthar pānī ho jānā, 1. The melting of a stony heart. 2. A difficult task to be facilitated. پتهرپسيجنا patthar pasījnā, To be softened or melted (a stony heart). پتهرچهاتي پررکهنا patthar chhātī par rakhnā, To be patient by compulsion or from being without remedy. پتهرساپهينک مارنا patthar sā phenk mārnā, To reply rashly without understanding the meaning of oneʼs question. پتهرسي سرپهوڙنايامارنا patthar se sir phoṛnā or mārnā, To teach or instruct a fool. پتهرهونا patthar honā, 1. n. f. 1. A part; portion; a division of a village; a division of land into separate portions or strips. 2. ( In law .) An original or separate share in a joint or coparcenary village or estate comprising many villages. 3. A strip of cloth; a bandage; plaster; fillet. 4. A narrow slip of paper or metal. 5. A head-band or narrow plate of silver or brass worn across the turban. 6. A dashing blade; a gay young spark. 7. A rafter, or the whole row or series of rafters that cover one side of a house. 8. A side of a bedstead. 1. Paṭṭi se paṭṭī milā-ke khāṭ bichhātī hai! Wom. Their beds are joined together side by side! (Such a loving couple as they are! Or, she is so closely watched). 2. Maiṅ kyā teṛī paṭṭī tale kī hūṅ . Wom. Prov. Am I in aught your inferior? 9. (S. पट् to go) A long straight run. Ghoṛe ko paṭṭī do . 10. Parting of the hair in the middle or side. 1. Kahīṅ apnī paṭṭī saṅvāre ko…
s. f. 1. A leaf. 2. Hemp, of which an intoxicating potion is made. See بهانگ bhāng.
s. f. (dakh.) A leaf of the cholam (Holcus saccharatus, Lin. ) held between the lips and sounded as a musical instrument (Qanoon-e-islam). ¶ patī (for पति ) s. m. Lord, master.

def: s.f. A piece or strip of cloth; bandage, ligature, swath, plaster, cataplasm; splints, band, fillet, ribbon, tape, brace, tie; head-band, a narrow plate of metal (worn across the turban by sepoys and their native officers); a rafter, or the whole row or series of rafters that cover one side of a house; side piece of the frame of a bedstead ( = pāṭī); parting of the hair in the middle; side-locks of hair over each temple; lath (of split bamboo, &c.); spokes of a wheel; a ledge; line, row; a rule to draw lines by; a small plank, board, tablet, table (as in 'plane-table'); a small rectangular board on which children write ( = pāṭī); a morsel of betel-leaf made up with betel-nut, lime, &c. ( = bīṛī; pān); a foil, &c. ( = paṭā); piece, part, portion; division of a village; division of land into portions or strips; holding or tenure in coparcenary; a written order or patent; a title to a certain tenure of land; list, statement, invoice, document (of any kind); public subscription paper; cess, assessment; — a kind of sweetmeat ( = pāṭī)
more: s. f. 1. ( पट्टी ) A bandage, a fillet. 2. ( पट्टिका ) A quarter of a place. 3. The side pieces of the frame of a bedstead. پٿي پکڙکي هلتي رهنا paṭṭī pakaṛke hilte rahnā, denotes excessive weakness and laziness. پٿي توڙنا paṭṭī toṛnā, To be bedrid. پٿي سي پٿي ملانا paṭṭī se paṭṭī milānā, expresses the most intimate proximity. 4. A row. 5. A kind of sweetmeat. 6. Division of the hair which is combed towards the two sides and divided by a line in the middle. 7. ( पट्ट or पट्टी ) A written order or patent.

s. (dakh.) A custom observed on the third day after a woman has been delivered: also, a ceremony sometimes practised before marriage. The side lock of hair over each temple ( Qanoon-e-islam ). Assessment, a rule to draw lines by, a cataplasm, bolt, knocker (of a door), felly of a wheel, lath, ledge (Harris). ¶ puṭī ( पुटी ) A cup or cavity made of a leaf folded or doubled, a plate or platter made of leaves, a basket, a hamper, a muzzle (to confine the mouth: Harris ).
subs: (-par), To apply a bandage, &c. (to a sore or wound); to bandage (the eyes), to blindfold

v.t. To teach the letters (to a child) from the paṭṭī or board; (fig.) to explain (anything to anyone), to give a lesson (to); to advise, counsel; to put (one) up to (some trick or roguery); to cheat, deceive, take in

n. f. S. पवन from पू to make clean. 1. Air; wind; ether; atmosphere. Paun meṅ paun mile, pānī meṅ pānī . Prov. Air with air mingling, water with water. (Dust to dust, ashes to ashes). 2. The breath of life; the spirit. Nau duāre kā pīṅjrā, jis meṅ panchhī, paun. Rahne kā hai āchraj, gaë achambā kaun? The Soul Kabīr. Nine doors, one cage, for birds within but air, The wonder is 'tis there, not flown elsewhere. 3. A spell; charm; magic. Sīyāne ne paun mārī. paun pānī kā asar . Effects of the climate. paun parichchhā , n. f. Ascertaining the direction of the wind. pavan-chakkī , More com. havā-chakkī , n. f. A windmill. paun ke pūt, paun-sut , n. m. lit . son of ether. ( Myth .) An epithet of Hanumān and Nāg (serpent).
1. s. f. Air, wind. 2. pawan, s. m. Regent of the winds and of the N.W. quarter. پون چکي pawan-chakkī, s. f. A windmill. پون کوپوت pawan kau pūt, Son of Pawan, i. e. Hanūmān.

r: pe, pa, Nāgarī, p, fe, fārsī, pārsī, b, poṅgā, boṅgā, upṭan, ubṭan, abjad, be, pa-kār
ety: It does not occur in the Arabic alphabet; but the letter fe is substituted for it in Persian words adopted into the Arabic language, thus fārsī for pārsī
def: s.m. The letter or sound pa
more: The third letter of the Hindustānī and Persian alphabets, and the first of the labial group ( pa-varg ), or the 21st of the Hindī alphabet. 1. In Sanskrit, as a substantive masculine it stands for 1. wind, 2. the god of wind, Aeolus . 2. It is occasionally found in composition as a contraction of pāṅch , as pa-serī . As an initial, medial, or final, it is interchangeable with b , as poṅgā = boṅgā a tube, phukrāṅd = bhukrāṅd bad smell, bhal bhal = phal phal sound of gushing water, chapak = chabak throb, P. tap = tab fever; with f (in Persian) as P. pīl = fīl elephant, sapaid = safaid white, kalap = kalaf starch; with k as phoklā = khoklā hollow, pakhālnā = khaṅgālnā wash out, upāṛnā = ukhāṛnā root up, ḍhap = ḍhak cover. As an initial and medial (in Persian), with j , as P. pālez = jālez a melon field, guptī = gujjī secret; with m , as H. poṭ = moṭ bundle, baṭpār = baṭmār a highwayman, P. …

the third letter of the Persian alphabet, corresponding to प in Sanskrit. It does not occur in the Arabic alphabet; but the letter ف fe is substituted for it in Persian words adopted into the Arabic language. With ه he affixed, it may stand for the Sanskrit फ as پهـ.
subs: vulg. pa-barg, s.m. The p series, the labial series of consonants.

def: v.t. To see, look, behold ( = dekhnā); to desire, wish for, long for.
more: v. n. S. प्रेक्षण, Pr. pekhaī ; from प्र, ईक्ष to see. 1. To see; look; behold ( dekhnā ). 2. To wish for; desire; long for. Dekhnā so pekhnā . Prov. To see is to desire. pekhnā , n. m. 1. A plaything; toy. 2. A puppet show; a raree show. Jag pekhan, tum dekhan-hāre; Bidhi, Hari, Shambhu nachāvan-hāre . Rāmāyan. This world's a puppet show, thou Ram look'st on, Brahm, Hari, and Shiv thou mak'st to dance hereon. 3. The arts and tricks of women.
s. m. Play, farce, sham, comedy.

def: s.m. A puppet-show; show, spectacle, play, comedy, farce, sham; illusion; — a plaything, toy; the wiles and artifices of woman.
s.m. Player, actor, &c.
more: s. m. A player, an actor.

def: s.m. See pelā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیل (pīl). Cognate with Pashto پيل (pil).

def: v. a. To shove, to push.
s.m. Shove, push; — s.f. Crush, crowd; abundance, plenty (see rel; and rel-pel)
more: (from پيلنا q. v.) s. m. Shove, push.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پیل (pīl). Cognate with Pashto پيل (pil).

def: s.m. A testicle ( = pelaṛ).
more: s. m. 1. A testicle. 2. Fault, oppression. 3. A prop, a support.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pittala), from Sanskrit पीतल (pītala, “brass, yellow”).

def: s.m. A prop, support; — oppression, tyranny; wrong, fault.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pittala), from Sanskrit पीतल (pītala, “brass, yellow”).

def: s.m. The ball or cocoon which the silk-worm forms round itself; the silkworm ( = kirm-pīla).
more: n. The ball or cocoon which the silkworm forms round itself. kirim pīlā , the silkworm.
n. m. P. pīl elephant. The bishop (in chess). pīl-band , n. m. A term in chess, signifying that a bishop and two pawns mutually support each other.
s. The ball or cocoon which the silkworm forms round itself, the silkworm.

def: E. n. m. A testicle.

ety: pel˚ = Prk. पेल्ल(इ); S. प्रेरय(ति), rt. ईर् with प्र; or S. पीड(ते), rt. पीड्
def: v.t. To shove, push, thrust, jostle; to impel, drive on forcibly, to push on; to push down, thrust in; to press, crush, squeeze; to stuff, cram, ram; to express, squeeze out.
more: E. v. n. S. पीडनं, Pr. पीलण pressing. 1. To express; squeeze out; press. Pakkā honā chāhe to pakke ke saṅg khel; Kachchī sarsoṅ pel-ke, kharī hoë nā tel . Prov. If you a wrestler strong would be, then wrestle with the strong, From unripe mustard seeds expressed nor oil nor oil-cakes wrung. 2. To shove; push; thrust in; penetrate. pel', pelā , n. m. A push; thrust; shove. pel'yā, peṛyā, peḍyā , n. m. The man who supplies the press with the cut cane, etc. to be expressed.
v. a. 1. To shove, to push. 2. To stuff, to cram. 3. To express, to squeeze out. پيل پال pel-pāl, part. Shoving, pushing, &c.

ety: Prk. पेलडं, पेलडअं; S. पेल+रं, and र+कं
def: s.m. A testicle.

ety: pel˚, rt. of pelnā, q.v.+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A pusher, shover; wrestler, athlete.
s.m. = prem, q.v. s.m. Love, affection, kindness, tender regard, kindliness, friendship
subs: adj. Devoted to the exercise of love
adj. & s.m. Intent on love, filled with affection, affectionate, loving, constant; — one who is constant in love, &c.
s.m. The quality or the essence of love
(-ke sāth), To be in love (with); to be fast friends (with)
adj. 'Coloured with the dye of love,' strongly attached (to), in love (with), enamoured (of)
s.m. 'Ocean of love'; the name of the tenth section of the Bhāgavat purāṇ, in which the love-sports of Kr̤ishṇa are related; an epithet of the Deity
adj. Connected with love, having relation to love
(-par), To show affection (for); to be enamoured (of)
s.f. A creeper; a parasitic plant
prem-vat, adj. Full of love, affectionate; — prem-vatī, s.f. Mistress, spouse
adj. Under the influence of love, over head and ears in love, deeply enamoured (of).

def: adj. & s.m. = premī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Loving, affectionate, friendly; — an affectionate person; a lover.
s.m. An imitative sound; singing, humming, buzzing; squeak, squeal; — peṅ, s.m. Pride, conceit
n. 1. A singing, buzzing sound; a squeak; squeal; squall. 2. ( peṅ ). Pride; boast. Ghaṛī bhar meṅ peṅ nikāl dūṅgā .

s.m. = peć, q.v. s.m. Turn, winding; revolution; involution; convolution; — twist, coil; plait, fold; — entanglement, complication, maze, perplexity, intricacy, ambiguity; hitch, difficulty, trouble; trick (in wrestling, &c.); artifice, stratagem; wiliness, craftiness; duplicity, deceit; — a roll (of cloth, &c.); a screw; a machine
more: W. n. f. A peacock's tail.
subs: s.m. & adj. Twist upon twist, all convolution; all intricacy, &c.; — involved, intricate, &c.
v.n. To experience trouble, suffer distress or loss
v.n. The twist (of anything) to become loose; a screw to come out
(-meṅ), A difficulty or hitch to arise (in); to occur as a difficulty or obstacle; to become complicated, difficult, or intricate; — to be entangled (in)
v.n. An artifice or stratagem to be successful; to prevail by stratagem, &c.
(-ke), To bind the twists (of a turban) with care
adj. Twisted, convoluted, coiled, spiral; winding, sinuous; intricate, complicated, involved; knotty, difficult; ambiguous; crooked, tortuous; artful, tricky, wily, crafty, deceitful
adj. Coil within coil; involved, entangled, intricate, complex
(-ko), To wind round, to twist; to screw; to circumvent, to deceive
(-meṅ), To entangle (the string of an adversary's paper kite); to throw obstacles or difficulties (in the way of)
(-meṅ; or -se), To practise artifices or tricks, to trick, deceive; to complicate; — to grapple (with, in wrestling)
v.n. To be twisted, &c.; to coil; to writhe; to be perplexed; to fall into difficulties, to sustain a loss
or khul-jānā, v.n. To be untwisted; to be unscrewed
(-se); To entangle (the silk of one's paper kite with that of another kite), to fight paper kites; — to meet, to embrace
peć-tāb, s.m. Twisting and twining; convolution, twisting knots, folds; contortions; restlessness, anxiety, agitation, perplexity, disquietude, distraction, distress; vexation, anger, indignation
v.n. To twist and turn; to writhe; to suffer distress or anxiety; to be vexed.
